title: calceolaria trilobata subsp. trilobata
intro: Calceolaria trilobata subsp. trilobata, also known as the Three-lobed Slipper Flower, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the Calceolaria genus and is native to the Andean region of South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Calceolaria trilobata subsp. trilobata include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and treat skin conditions such as wounds and eczema. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be consumed as teas or used topically as creams and ointments.
